title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 2 June 2026.

items.title: **US bill** requiring paid games to be playable after they shut down has passed initial vote and moved to the Senate
bullets: Should the bill become law in its current state without any modifications, the publisher of a game whose live services are ended would be legally required to do one of these three things:

bullets.text: Provide an alternate version of the game that “can be used by the purchaser independent of services controlled by the operator” (i.e. a version that no longer needs to connect to the server)
Patch or update the existing game so it can continue to be playable “independent of services controlled by the operator” after the servers have been shut down.
Refund the player the full cost they originally paid for the game

bullets: These rules would only affect games released after January 1st, 2027 and would not apply to subscription-based games or games that are “offered to a person for no monetary consideration," i.e. free-to-play games .etc.

items.title: Sony **State of Play** June 2026 Tonight. 10pm BST / 11pm CEST / 5pm EDT / 2pm PDT
**New Zealand** games sector hits $1bn revenue two years earlier than anticipated. Growth is attributed to the government's Game Development Sector Rebate and Centre of Digital Excellence funding
**Atari** acquires Crossy Road developer Hipster Whale. Australian studio purchased for an initial $29.3 million, with an earn-out that could push the total cost to $40 million
